name: Agent Failure-Mode Audit
position: Runtime contracts for production agents that make unsupported operational claims.

buyer.icp: Teams running LLM agents in production that cannot produce receipts for send, deploy, publish, verify, change, or state-confirmation claims.
trigger_failures: agent said done but artifact did not exist
agent claimed an email or post was sent without a message receipt
agent cited a commit, URL, or state that did not resolve
agent repeated a corrected behavior after the correction was added to the prompt
agent ran unattended and reported success without an independently checked outcome

proof.honest_boundary: The mechanism is proven on Shadow itself; the next proof target is one outside agent stack.

offers.name: One-Failure Triage
offers.input: One concrete production-agent failure with a log, trace, receipt, screenshot, or public link.

offers.name: Failure Census
offers.input: Seven days of agent transcripts, run logs, or execution traces.
output: scored inventory of unverified completion claims
top 5 deterministic gates to install first

offers.name: Contract Install
offers.input: One agent stack, one repo, one production workflow.
output: receipt schema for completion, publish, deploy, send, and state-confirmation claims
tests proving each gate fires on the failure examples

offers.name: Governed Operations Retainer
offers.input: A team that continues shipping new agent behavior.
output: new gate design as failures appear

falsifier: If the free triage does not produce at least one concrete external failure submission, reply, or pricing conversation within seven days of publication, rewrite the offer around fabricated completion claims only.
